nodes.label: P5.4 — the readout MVP: per-depth heads Pareto-dominate all-tap
nodes.rationale: Per-depth heads beat all-tap on composite tasks (headroom +0.047 5/5 at 0.29x cost, mixed +0.021 5/5) and tie on flat at 8x cheaper; mechanism is all-tap diluting the class signal with drifted deep layers; adopt linear head as the deployable base.

nodes.label: P5.4 verdict: per-depth heads Pareto-dominate all-tap; ADOPT as the readout base
nodes.rationale: With readout parameters matched (heads 26544 approx all-tap 24740), the best single-depth head beats all-tap on headroom (+0.047, 5/5, 0.29x cost) and mixed (+0.021, 5/5), and ties on flat at 0.12x cost; a linear head approx MLP head, so a linear head is the deployable base.

nodes.label: Mechanism: all-tap dilutes the class signal by concatenating drifted tunnel layers
nodes.rationale: The decayed deep layers are not dead, they are misleading — correlated-but-drifted; a capacity-limited all-tap readout cannot cleanly zero-weight them, so it dilutes the class direction, while a single head reading one clean depth sidesteps the tunnel entirely — the spine made operational.
